Package org.apache.juneau.commons.inject

Interface BeanStore

All Known Subinterfaces:
WritableBeanStore
All Known Implementing Classes:
BasicBeanStore, SpringBeanStore, StackOverlay
public interface BeanStore
Spring-bean-like interface for looking up beans by type and name.

This interface provides a minimal contract for bean lookup operations, similar to Spring's BeanFactory interface.

Implementations of this interface can be backed by various sources:

  • Spring's ApplicationContext
  • Custom in-memory stores
  • Other dependency injection frameworks
Example:

BeanStore beanStore = ...; // Get bean by type Optional<MyService> service = beanStore.getBean(MyService.class); // Get bean by name Optional<Object> bean = beanStore.getBean("myBean"); // Get bean by type and name Optional<MyService> namedService = beanStore.getBean(MyService.class, "primary"); // Get all beans of a type Map<String, MyService> allServices = beanStore.getBeansOfType(MyService.class);

See Also:
  • Spring Framework - The IoC Container

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description

    createBeanFromMethod(Class<T> beanType, Object onClassOrObject)

    Returns true if this store can satisfy every parameter of the specified executable.
    boolean

    Returns true if this store contains at least one bean of the specified type.
    boolean

    Returns true if this store contains a bean of the specified type and name.
    default <T> T
    instantiate(Class<T> type)

  • Method Details

    • getBean

      <T> Optional<T> getBean(Class<T> beanType)
      Returns a bean of the specified type.

      If multiple beans of the specified type exist, the behavior is implementation-dependent. Typically, implementations will return the primary bean or throw an exception.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The bean type.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The bean type.
      Returns:
      The bean, or Optional.empty() if no bean of the specified type exists.

    • getBean

      <T> Optional<T> getBean(Class<T> beanType, String name)
      Returns a bean of the specified type and name.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The bean type.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The bean type.
      name - The bean name. Can be null.
      Returns:
      The bean, or Optional.empty() if no bean of the specified type and name exists.

    • getBeansOfType

      <T> Map<String,T> getBeansOfType(Class<T> beanType)
      Returns all beans of the specified type, keyed by bean name.

      The map keys are the bean names, and the values are the bean instances. If no beans of the specified type exist, returns an empty map.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The bean type.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The bean type.
      Returns:
      A map of bean names to bean instances. Never null.

    • hasBean

      boolean hasBean(Class<?> beanType)
      Returns true if this store contains at least one bean of the specified type.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The bean type.
      Returns:
      true if this store contains at least one bean of the specified type.

    • hasBean

      boolean hasBean(Class<?> beanType, String name)
      Returns true if this store contains a bean of the specified type and name.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The bean type.
      name - The bean name. Can be null.
      Returns:
      true if this store contains a bean of the specified type and name.

    • instantiate

      default <T> T instantiate(Class<T> type)
      Convenience shortcut for instantiating a bean of the specified type using this store.

      Equivalent to:

      BeanInstantiator.of(type, this).run()

      Use this when you don't need any of BeanInstantiator's configuration options (subtype, factory-method names, builder hooks, etc.). For more control over instantiation, use BeanInstantiator.of(Class, BeanStore) directly.

      Example:

      // Instantiate a bean using this store for parameter resolution MyService service = beanStore.instantiate(MyService.class);

      Type Parameters:
      T - The bean type.
      Parameters:
      type - The bean type to instantiate.
      Returns:
      The instantiated bean.

    • getBeanSupplier

      <T> Optional<Supplier<T>> getBeanSupplier(Class<T> beanType)
      Returns the supplier for an unnamed bean of the specified type.

      If no supplier is found in this store, searches the parent store recursively.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The bean type.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The bean type.
      Returns:
      The supplier, or Optional.empty() if no supplier of the specified type exists.

    • getBeanSupplier

      <T> Optional<Supplier<T>> getBeanSupplier(Class<T> beanType, String name)
      Returns the supplier for a named bean of the specified type.

      If no supplier with the specified name is found in this store, searches the parent store recursively.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The bean type.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The bean type.
      name - The bean name. Can be null.
      Returns:
      The supplier, or Optional.empty() if no supplier of the specified type and name exists.

    • createBeanFromMethod

      default <T> Optional<T> createBeanFromMethod(Class<T> beanType, Object onClassOrObject, Predicate<MethodInfo> filter, Object... extraBeans)
      Finds and invokes a factory method that produces a bean of type beanType.

      Scans all public methods of the resource class identified by onClassOrObject:

      • If onClassOrObject is a Class, only static methods are eligible and the instance parameter passed to the invoked method is null.
      • If onClassOrObject is any other object, both instance and static methods on its class are eligible, and the object itself is passed as the receiver.

      The first method satisfying all of the following is invoked:

      1. Not deprecated.
      2. Return type is assignment-compatible with beanType.
      3. Accepted by filter (if non-null; otherwise any method qualifies).
      4. All parameters can be resolved from this store plus any extraBeans.

      The default implementation returns Optional.empty(). Override in concrete stores (see BasicBeanStore) to enable factory-method scanning.

      Example:

      // Filter only beanStore.createBeanFromMethod(CallLogger.class, resource, RestContext::isBeanMethod) .ifPresent(creator::impl); // Filter + extra bean not yet in the store beanStore.createBeanFromMethod(EncoderSet.class, resource, RestContext::isBeanMethod, builder) .ifPresent(x -> builder.impl(x)); // No filter, no extra beans beanStore.createBeanFromMethod(CallLogger.class, resource);

      Type Parameters:
      T - The bean type.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The type of bean to create. Must not be null.
      onClassOrObject - The object instance or Class whose public methods are searched. Must not be null.
      filter - Optional predicate restricting which methods are eligible. Can be null.
      extraBeans - Optional bean instances visible to parameter resolution for this call only. These are not registered in the store.
      Returns:
      The created bean wrapped in an Optional, or Optional.empty() if no matching factory method was found.
      Throws:
      BeanCreationException - If a matching method was found but threw an exception during invocation.
      See Also:
      • BeanInstantiator — for instantiating a bean from its own constructors, builders, or factory methods

    • createBeanFromMethod

      default <T> Optional<T> createBeanFromMethod(Class<T> beanType, Object onClassOrObject)
      Convenience overload of createBeanFromMethod(Class, Object, Predicate, Object...) with no filter and no extra beans.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The bean type.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The type of bean to create. Must not be null.
      onClassOrObject - The object instance or Class whose public methods are searched. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The created bean wrapped in an Optional, or Optional.empty() if no matching factory method was found.
      Throws:
      BeanCreationException - If a matching method was found but threw an exception during invocation.

    • getBeanType

      default <T> Optional<Class<? extends T>> getBeanType(Class<T> beanType)
      Returns the implementation class registered for the specified bean type, if any.

      Type bindings let callers register a default implementation class to be instantiated when an actual bean of beanType is needed. Bindings are inherited through the parent chain.

      The default implementation returns Optional.empty() (no binding). Override in concrete stores (see BasicBeanStore) to enable type binding.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The bean type.
      Parameters:
      beanType - The bean type whose implementation class is being looked up.
      Returns:
      The implementation class, or Optional.empty() if no binding exists.

    • hasAllParams

      default boolean hasAllParams(ExecutableInfo executable, Object outer)
      Returns true if this store can satisfy every parameter of the specified executable.

      Skips:

      • The first parameter when it is an instance of outer (inner-class outer reference).
      • Parameters typed as Optional (always considered satisfied).
      Parameters:
      executable - The constructor or method to inspect.
      outer - The outer object to use for inner-class param resolution. Can be null.
      Returns:
      true if every required parameter can be resolved from this store.

    • getParams

      default Object[] getParams(ExecutableInfo executable, Object outer)
      Resolves every parameter of the specified executable from this store.

      For Optional-typed parameters the wrapped Optional is returned directly; for all others the bean instance (or null when missing) is returned. See hasAllParams(org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ExecutableInfo, java.lang.Object) for the full skip rules.

      Parameters:
      executable - The constructor or method to resolve parameters for.
      outer - The outer object to use for inner-class param resolution. Can be null.
      Returns:
      An array of resolved parameter values, one per parameter of executable.

    • getMissingParams

      default String getMissingParams(ExecutableInfo executable, Object outer)
      Returns a comma-delimited list of parameter type names that cannot be resolved from this store.

      Intended for diagnostic error messages — see hasAllParams(org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ExecutableInfo, java.lang.Object) for the exact match rules. Skipped parameters (outer-instance match, Optional) never appear in the result.

      Parameters:
      executable - The constructor or method to inspect.
      outer - The outer object to use for inner-class param resolution. Can be null.
      Returns:
      A comma-delimited list of missing parameter type names, or null when none are missing.
